reasoning_model_1: The model fails to strictly adhere to the content requirements. First, it translates the English word 'market' in the prompt to '市场' (00:01), failing the code-switching test. More critically, it hallucinates the word '同比' (year-on-year) at 00:04 ('...销售额同比增长了...'), which alters the meaning of the script by adding specific data qualification not present in the source text. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Professional and Objective' (专业客观), the model's performance is subpar. The tone is somewhat casual, fast-paced, and has an upbeat, enthusiastic inflection, particularly noticeable in the rising pitch at the end of the sentence (00:05 '百分之十五'). This sounds more like a sales pitch or a casual conversation than a neutral, objective business report.
reasoning_model_2: The model performs well in terms of style but has a minor content deviation. Like Model 1, it translates the English word 'market' to '市场' (00:01), which technically violates the 'Content Accuracy' requirement for a perfect score, as the script contained mixed-lingual text. However, unlike Model 1, it does not add any extra words, maintaining the integrity of the remaining text. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Professional and Objective', the model is excellent. The voice is deep, steady, and calm. The prosody features a measured pace and a flat/downward intonation at the end (00:05 '百分之十五'), which perfectly conveys a sense of authority, neutrality, and professionalism suitable for a market data report.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. While both models failed to read the specific English word 'market' (translating it to Chinese), Model 2 was otherwise accurate, whereas Model 1 hallucinated an additional word ('同比') that changed the semantic meaning of the statistics. In terms of the target style 'Professional and Objective', Model 2 provided a spot-on performance with a steady, neutral, and authoritative news-anchor tone. In contrast, Model 1 sounded too casual, rushed, and enthusiastic, lacking the necessary objectivity and gravitas. Model 2 receives a higher score, though capped at 2 due to the content normalization error.
